title: Permanent Presence under Uncertainty
subtitle: A Partially Observable Game-Theoretic Framework for Unmanned Systems, Cost–Frequency Dynamics, and Strategic Stability
title_normalized: permanent presence under uncertainty: a partially observable game-theoretic framework for unmanned systems, cost-frequency dynamics, and strategic stability

creators.name: Wu, Shaoyuan

Short form of the working paper title

description: This working paper develops a partially observable game-theoretic framework for unmanned systems that reframes permanent presence as a strategic condition under uncertainty rather than a platform-centric capability. It formalizes permanent presence through a Partially Observable Stochastic Game (POSG), extends Cost–Distance–Frequency (CDF) logic by treating frequency as an adversary-facing random variable, and links observability, tempo, survivability, entropy, and verification to strategic stability and AI-enabled arms control.
abstract: The operational advantage of unmanned systems is increasingly derived not from superior precision or predictive accuracy, but from their ability to sustain presence while resisting low-cost opponent inference. This paper develops a formal framework that conceptualizes permanent presence as a strategic condition rather than a platform-centric capability, reframing it as a portfolio under uncertainty. Building on a Partially Observable Stochastic Game (POSG) formulation, the analysis models the structural tension between observability, operational tempo, and survivability, and extends a Cost–Distance–Frequency (CDF) framework by treating frequency as an adversary-facing random variable rather than a controllable constant. The paper further explores the implications of this shift for strategic stability, verification, and AI-enabled arms control, arguing that uncertainty itself has become a central stabilizing—and destabilizing—factor in contemporary unmanned conflict.

series_information.series_description: Working Paper F-Series develops applied analytical frameworks for conflict, defense technology, AI-enabled operations, and strategic assessment.

methodology.core_problem: Permanent unmanned presence must resist low-cost adversarial inference; operational tempo and frequency should be modeled as adversary-facing random variables rather than controllable constants.
central_claims: Survivability and effectiveness are increasingly governed by uncertainty management rather than precision maximization.
Frequency and tempo should be modeled as adversary-facing random variables.
Strategic stability and verification increasingly depend on information structure rather than platform counts or static deployment zones.

variables.name: Spatial state
variables.description: Location or distribution, partially hidden from the adversary.

variables.name: Mission or tempo state
variables.description: Activity level, operational windows, and rhythm.

variables.name: Adversary capability and intent state
variables.description: Threat intensity, strike timing, and resource levels.

variables.name: Environmental state
variables.description: Weather, terrain masking, spectrum background, and logistical constraints.

main_formulas.name: Observation model
main_formulas.description: Observations are generated by a function that depends on both players' actions, making information quality endogenous to strategy.

main_formulas.name: Adversarial belief state
main_formulas.description: The adversary maintains a belief distribution over hidden system states.

main_formulas.name: Belief entropy
main_formulas.description: Entropy is used as a model-agnostic proxy for adversarial uncertainty.

main_formulas.name: Permanent presence portfolio
main_formulas.description: Presence is modeled as a weighted configuration over deterrent presence, survivable reserve, mobile uncertainty, and temporal randomization.

main_formulas.name: Long-term survivability objective
main_formulas.description: Optimization objective prioritizing long-term survivability and sustained effects rather than single-episode payoff.

main_formulas.name: Frequency as random variable
main_formulas.description: Frequency is treated as a random variable from the opponent's perspective.

main_formulas.name: Adversarial reconnaissance investment
main_formulas.description: Adversarial investment reduces uncertainty subject to an irreducible uncertainty floor.

main_formulas.name: Operational value with belief entropy
main_formulas.formula: Value = V(D, F) − C − α · Exposure + β · H(b_t^Red)
main_formulas.description: Adversarial uncertainty becomes a positive strategic output.

main_formulas.name: Presence efficiency
main_formulas.description: Presence efficiency as a function of the presence portfolio and environmental state.

main_formulas.name: Capability constraint
main_formulas.description: Constraint on portfolio configuration under available capability resources.

main_formulas.name: Expected risk
main_formulas.description: Expected loss conditioned on adversarial belief and frequency regime.

presence_modes.name: Mobile uncertainty
presence_modes.description: Spatial ambiguity through movement or distributed positioning.

presence_modes.name: Temporal randomization
presence_modes.description: Rhythmic ambiguity that resists cheap timing inference.

governance_levers: Assured human accountability through traceable responsibility chains
Signal management norms regulating manipulative informational behavior
Audit-by-design requirements embedded at the system-design stage

dimensions.name: Spatial Ambiguity
dimensions.description: Hidden or partially observable location/distribution of unmanned capabilities.

dimensions.name: Tempo Uncertainty
dimensions.description: Randomized activity level, frequency, and operational rhythm observed by the adversary as uncertain variables.

dimensions.name: Adversarial Belief
dimensions.description: Opponent belief distribution over possible system states conditioned on observations and past actions.

dimensions.name: Belief Entropy
dimensions.description: Proxy for adversarial uncertainty and cost of inference.

dimensions.name: Reconnaissance Investment
dimensions.description: Adversarial resource expenditure to reduce uncertainty and improve localization/timing inference.

dimensions.name: Structural Uncertainty Floor
dimensions.description: Irreducible lower bound of uncertainty that remains despite adversarial investment.

dimensions.name: Presence Portfolio
dimensions.description: Weighted configuration of visible, latent, spatially ambiguous, and temporally randomized presence modes.

dimensions.name: Presence Efficiency
dimensions.description: Effectiveness of a presence configuration under environmental and operational constraints.

dimensions.name: Expected Risk
dimensions.description: Expected loss conditioned on adversarial belief state and frequency regime.

key_findings: Unmanned system advantage increasingly derives from sustained presence and resistance to low-cost adversarial inference, not only from precision or predictive accuracy.
Permanent presence is not continuous activity or fixed deployment; it is credible persistence under adversarial observation where timing and location cannot be cheaply resolved.
Observability and coordination create a structural tradeoff: they improve short-term efficiency but increase exposure, while randomized tempo reduces efficiency but improves survivability.
A POSG formulation is more appropriate than a single-agent POMDP because uncertainty is strategically generated and manipulated by both sides.
Frequency should be modeled as an adversary-facing random variable, with variance becoming a strategic lever under cost–frequency dynamics.
Belief entropy converts adversarial uncertainty into a positive strategic output within operational value calculations.
Strategic stability becomes an information-structural problem involving persistence, verification difficulty, and misperception risk rather than a purely material balance problem.
AI governance and arms control must shift toward process-based compliance, traceable responsibility chains, signal-management norms, and audit-by-design.

disclaimer: This metadata record is generated from the uploaded PDF and early ORCID-derived metadata. The working paper develops a formal analytical framework for unmanned systems, uncertainty management, and strategic stability; it does not prescribe operational deployment choices or provide tactical instructions.
